close_reason: Positive observation: Arc only on RSA variant is correct
description:
LocalKey::Ed25519 and LocalKey::P256 hold the SigningKey directly; LocalKey::RsaPss2048 wraps it in Arc. This LOOKS like inconsistent enum design that should be normalized.

It is INTENTIONAL: the Arc exists only because RSA signing is dispatched to spawn_blocking (lib.rs:186-205) and the closure must own its key. Cloning the Arc is a pointer copy. Ed25519 and P-256 sign synchronously in-thread (sub-µs to ~10 µs) and never need to clone the key, so a bare value is correct.

If a future change adds spawn_blocking to Ed25519/P-256 signing (e.g. for unconditional fairness), the Arc would need to be added there too — but doing it preemptively pays the atomic-refcount cost on every fast-path signature. Don't.

DEFEND.
title: DEFEND: Arc<rsa::pss::SigningKey> only on the RSA variant (asymmetric LocalKey enum)
